titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Earl Rosenblum papers
titlestmt.author: Katherine Isham

publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday February 2, 2026 at 22:00

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is unprocessed, but may be requested by contacting the Medical Historical Library at historical.library@yale.edu.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Barbara Rosenblum Kryger, Renee King, and Lesley Snitger, 2025.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Earl Rosenblum papers. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection contains materials related to Dr. Earl Rosenblum's (1911-2008) service as Lieutenant Colonel in the US Army, Ninth Air Force, Medical Corps. The collection also includes a small amount of material related to his medical training and career as an abdominal surgeon after the war. Approximately half of the collection consists of typescript pages with attached photographs, photocopies, and a bound copy of Rosenblum's "Memoirs, to March, 1946," which describes his time in the military including being present during the liberation of Paris. Meir Kryger, Rosenblum's son in law, assisted with the creation of the memoirs. Materials in the collection include military documents and reports, photographic prints and negative strips, original and photocopied newspaper clippings, correspondence, reprints of medical journal articles authored by Rosenblum, a diary, and typescripts and a bound copy of his memoirs.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

corpname.part: United States. Army. Medical Service Corps
